questions.question_text: An agent sells goods of value of ₹ 15000. The commission which he receives at the rate of $1\frac{1}{2}\%$ is
questions.solution:
Commission = $12\frac{1}{2}\%$ of ₹ $15000$
= ₹ $\left(\frac{25}{2} \times \frac{1}{100} \times 15000\right) = ₹ 1875$.

questions.question_text: One-eighth of a number is 41.5. What will 69% of that number be?
questions.solution:
Let the number be $x$.
Then, $\frac{1}{8}x = 41.5 \Rightarrow x = 41.5 \times 8 = 332$.
$\therefore 69\%$ of $332 = \left(\frac{69}{100} \times 332\right) = 229.08$.

questions.question_text: Ten percent of twenty plus twenty percent of ten equals
60% of 264 is the same as
270 candidates appeared for an examination, of which 252 passed. The pass percentage is:
questions.solution: Pass percentage = $\left(\frac{252}{270} \times 100\right)\% = \frac{280}{3}\% = 93\frac{1}{3}\%$.

questions.question_text: 5 out of 2250 parts of earth is sulphur. What is the percentage of sulphur in earth?
questions.solution: Required percentage = $\left(\frac{5}{2250} \times 100\right)\% = \frac{2}{9}\%$.

questions.question_text: In an examination Sumit scores a total of 626 marks out of 850. What is his approximate percentage in the examination?
questions.solution: Required percentage = $\left(\frac{626}{850} \times 100\right)\% = \frac{1252}{17}\% \approx 73.65\% \approx 74\%$.

questions.passage_text:
The marks obtained by a student are given below. What is his total percentage in all the subjects?

| Subjects | Total Marks | Marks Obtained |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Mathematics | 100 | 100 |
| Science | 100 | 84 |
| Social Studies | 100 | 67 |
| English | 50 | 25 |
| Hindi | 50 | 24 |
questions.question_text: What is his total percentage in all the subjects?
questions.solution:
Required percentage = $\left(\frac{100 + 84 + 67 + 25 + 24}{100 + 100 + 100 + 50 + 50} \times 100\right)\%$
$= \left(\frac{300}{400} \times 100\right)\% = 75\%$.

questions.passage_text:
The following table gives the marks obtained by a student in the first and second semester examinations in four subjects in a given year.

| Subjects | 1st Semester | 2nd Semester | Maximum Marks |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Physics | 35 | 30 | 50 |
| Chemistry | 30 | 25 | 50 |
| Mathematics | 65 | 45 | 100 |
| Social Science | 80 | 85 | 100 |
questions.question_text: The aggregate percentage of marks obtained by the student in the given year is nearly
questions.solution:
Total marks obtained = $(35 + 30 + 30 + 25 + 65 + 45 + 80 + 85) = 395$.
Maximum marks = $(50 \times 2 + 50 \times 2 + 100 \times 2 + 100 \times 2) = 600$.
$\therefore$ Percentage of marks = $\left(\frac{395}{600} \times 100\right)\% \approx 65.8\% \approx 66\%$.

questions.question_text: I paid ₹ 27.20 as sales tax on a watch worth ₹ 340. Find the rate of sales tax.
questions.solution: Rate of sales tax = $\left(\frac{27.20}{340} \times 100\right)\% = 8\%$.

questions.question_text: What percent of 88 is 33?
questions.solution: Required percentage = $\left(\frac{33}{88} \times 100\right)\% = \frac{75}{2}\% = 37.5\%$.

questions.question_text: 0.01 is what percent of 0.1?
questions.solution: Required percentage = $\left(\frac{0.01}{0.1} \times 100\right)\% = 10\%$.

questions.question_text: What percent is 1 minute and 12 seconds of an hour?
questions.solution:
$1 \text{ min } 12 \text{ sec} = \frac{12}{60} \text{ min} = \frac{1}{5} \text{ min} = \frac{6}{5} \text{ min}$.
$1 \text{ hour} = 60 \text{ min}$.
$\therefore$ Required percentage = $\left(\frac{6/5}{60} \times 100\right)\% = 2\%$.

questions.question_text: The enrolment of students in a school increases from 560 to 581. What is the percent increase in the enrolment?
questions.solution: Percentage increase = $\left(\frac{21}{560} \times 100\right)\% = \frac{15}{4}\% = 3.75\%$.

questions.question_text: What percent of 7.2 kg is 18 gms?
questions.solution: Required percentage = $\left(\frac{18}{7200} \times 100\right)\% = \frac{1}{4}\% = 0.25\%$.

questions.question_text: What percent of ₹ 2650 is ₹ 1987.50?
questions.solution: Required percentage = $\left(\frac{1987.50}{2650} \times 100\right)\% = \left(\frac{19875}{265} \times \frac{1}{100} \times 100\right)\% = 75\%$.

questions.question_text: What percent of a day is 3 hours?
questions.solution: Required percentage = $\left(\frac{3}{24} \times 100\right)\% = \frac{25}{2}\% = 12\frac{1}{2}\%$.

questions.question_text: The price for a pair of cuff links is ₹ 1.00. The price for a 5-pair package of cuff links is ₹ 3.40. The 5-pair package is what percent cheaper per pair than 5 pairs purchased separately?
questions.solution:
Price of 5 pairs when purchased separately = ₹ $5$.
Price of 5 pair package = ₹ $3.40$.
Difference in price = ₹ $(5 - 3.40) = ₹ 1.60$.
$\therefore$ Required percentage = $\left(\frac{1.6}{5} \times 100\right)\% = 32\%$.

questions.question_text: A bakery opened with its daily supply of 40 dozen rolls. Half of the rolls were sold by noon and 60% of the remaining rolls were sold between noon and closing time. How many dozen rolls were left unsold?
questions.solution:
Number of rolls sold by noon = $\frac{1}{2}$ of 40 dozen = 20 dozen.
Number of rolls sold between noon and closing time
= 60% of 20 dozen = $\left(\frac{60}{100}\times20\right)$ dozen = 12 dozen.
Number of rolls left unsold = [40 - (20 + 12)] dozen = 8 dozen.

questions.question_text: 5 kg of metal A and 20 kg of metal B are mixed to form an alloy. The percentage of metal A in the alloy is
questions.solution:
Weight of metal A = 5 kg.
Total weight of the alloy = (5 + 20) kg = 25 kg.
\ Required percentage = $\left(\frac{5}{25}\times100\right)\% = 20\%$.

questions.question_text: 30% apples out of 450 are rotten. How many apples are in good condition?
questions.solution:
Number of rotten apples = 30% of 450 = $\left(\frac{30}{100}\times450\right) = 135$.
\ Number of good apples = (450 - 135) = 315.

questions.question_text: A company pays rent of ₹ 25000 per month for office space to its owner. But if the company pays the annual rent at the beginning of the year the owner gives a discount of 5% on the total annual rent. What is the annual amount the company pays to the owner after the discount?
questions.solution:
Total annual rent = ₹ (25000 $\times$ 12) = ₹ 300000.
Discount = 5% of ₹ 300000 = ₹ $\left(\frac{5}{100}\times300000\right) = ₹ 15000$.
\ Annual rent paid after discount = ₹ (300000 - 15000) = ₹ 285000.

questions.question_text: An interval of 3 hours 40 minutes is wrongly estimated as 3 hours 45.5 minutes. The error percentage is
questions.solution:
Correct time = 3 hrs 40 min = (3 $\times$ 60 + 40) min = 220 min.
Error = 5.5 min.
\ Error % = $\left(\frac{5.5}{220}\times100\right)\% = \frac{5}{2}\% = 2.5\%$.

questions.passage_text:
The following table gives the income distribution of 200 households of a village:

| Monthly Income (in ₹) | Number of Households |
| :--- | :--- |
| < 1000 | 25 |
| < 2000 | 80 |
| < 5000 | 170 |
| < 10000 | 200 |
questions.question_text: What is the percentage of households whose monthly income is above ₹ 2000 but below ₹ 5000?
questions.solution:
Total number of households = 200.
Number of households whose monthly income is above ₹ 2000 but below ₹ 5000 = (170 - 80) = 90.
\ Required percentage = $\left(\frac{90}{200}\times100\right)\% = 45\%$.

questions.question_text: In two successive years 100 and 75 students of a school appeared at the final examination. Respectively 75% and 60% of them passed. The average rate of pass is
questions.solution:
Total number of students = (100 + 75) = 175.
Number of students passed = 75% of 100 + 60% of 75
= 75 + 45 = 120.
\ Pass percentage = $\left(\frac{120}{175}\times100\right)\% = \left(\frac{480}{7}\right)\% = 68\frac{4}{7}\%$.

questions.question_text: A toy merchant announces 25% rebate in prices of balls. If one needs to have a rebate of ₹ 40, then how many balls each costing ₹ 32, he should purchase?
questions.solution:
Rebate on one ball = 25% of ₹ 32 = ₹ $\left(\frac{25}{100}\times32\right) = ₹ 8$.
\ Required number of balls = $\frac{40}{8} = 5$.

questions.question_text: 1.14 expressed as a percent of 1.9 is
questions.solution:
Required percentage = $\left(\frac{1.14}{1.9}\times100\right)\%$
= $\left(\frac{114}{190}\times100\right)\% = 60\%$.

questions.question_text: Kamal has 160 toffees. He gave 5% toffees to Ravi, 15% toffees to Anita and one-fourth of the toffees to Gagan. How many toffees are left with Kamal after the distribution?
questions.solution:
Number of toffees distributed
= 5% of 160 + 15% of 160 + $\frac{1}{4}$ of 160
= $\left(\frac{5}{100}\times160\right) + \left(\frac{15}{100}\times160\right) + \left(160\times\frac{1}{4}\right)$
= 8 + 24 + 40 = 72.
\ Number of toffees left behind = 160 - 72 = 88.

questions.question_text: A shopkeeper sells note-books at the rate of ₹ 45 each and earns a commission of 4%. He also sells pencil boxes at the rate of ₹ 80 each and earns a commission of 20%. How much amount of commission will he earn in two weeks if he sells 10 note-books and 6 pencil boxes a day?
questions.solution:
Revenue obtained from sale of notebooks in 2 weeks
= ₹ (45 $\times$ 10 $\times$ 14) = ₹ 6300.
Revenue obtained from sale of pencil boxes in 2 weeks
= ₹ (80 $\times$ 6 $\times$ 14) = ₹ 6720.
\ Total commission earned
= 4% of ₹ 6300 + 20% of ₹ 6720
= 252 + 1344 = 1596.

questions.question_text: It costs ₹ 1 to photocopy a sheet of paper. However, 2% discount is allowed on all photocopies done after first 1000 sheets. How much will it cost to copy 5000 sheets of paper?
A housewife saved ₹ 2.50 in buying an item on sale. If she spent ₹ 25 for the item, approximately how much percent she saved in the transaction?
How many litres of pure acid are there in 8 litres of a 20% solution?
questions.solution:
Quantity of pure acid = 20% of 8 litres = $\left(\frac{20}{100}\times8\right)$ litres
= 1.6 litres.

questions.question_text: Rajeev buys goods worth ₹ 6650. He gets a rebate of 6% on it. After getting the rebate, he pays sales tax @ 10%. Find the amount he will have to pay for the goods.
Which one of the following shows the best percentage?

In a class of 65 students and 4 teachers, each student got sweets that are 20% of the total number of students and each teacher got sweets that are 40% of the total number of students. How many sweets are there?
questions.solution:
Number of sweets obtained by each student
= 20% of 65 = 13.
Number of sweets obtained by each teacher
= 40% of 65 = 26.
\ Total number of sweets
= 65 $\times$ 13 + 4 $\times$ 26 = 845 + 104 = 949.

questions.question_text: A student scores 55% marks in 8 papers of 100 marks each. He scores 15% of his total marks in English. How much does he score in English?
questions.solution:
Total marks obtained by the student = 55% of 800
= $\left(\frac{55}{100}\times800\right) = 440$.
\ Marks scored in English
= 15% of 440 = $\left(\frac{15}{100}\times440\right) = 66$.

questions.question_text: A, B, C and D have ₹ 40, 50, 60 and 70 respectively when they go to visit a fair. A spends ₹ 18, B spends ₹ 21, C spends ₹ 24 and D spends ₹ 27. Who has done the highest expenditure proportionate to his resources?
questions.solution:
Percentage of A’s expenditure = $\left(\frac{18}{40}\times100\right)\% = 45\%$.
Percentage of B’s expenditure = $\left(\frac{21}{50}\times100\right)\% = 42\%$.
Percentage of C’s expenditure = $\left(\frac{24}{60}\times100\right)\% = 40\%$.
Percentage of D’s expenditure = $\left(\frac{27}{70}\times100\right)\% = 38\frac{4}{7}\%$.
Clearly, A has done the highest expenditure.

questions.question_text: A country follows a progressive taxation system under which the income tax rate applicable varies for different slabs of income. Total tax is computed by calculating the tax for each slab and adding them up. The rates applicable are as follows:
40% of 60% of $\frac{3}{5}$th of a number is 504. What is 25% of $\frac{2}{5}$th of that number?
questions.solution:
Let the number be $x$.
Then, $40\%$ of $60\%$ of $\frac{3}{5}$ of $x=504$
$\Rightarrow \left(\frac{40}{100}\times\frac{60}{100}\times\frac{3}{5}\times x\right)=504$
$\Rightarrow \frac{18}{125}x=504$
$\Rightarrow x=\frac{504\times125}{18}=3500$.
$\therefore 25\%$ of $\frac{2}{5}$ of $3500=\left(\frac{25}{100}\times\frac{2}{5}\times3500\right)=350$.

Let $125\%$ of $3060-85\%$ of $x=408$. Then,
$85\%$ of $x=\left(\frac{125}{100}\times3060\right)-408$
$\Rightarrow \frac{85}{100}x=3825-408$
$\Rightarrow \frac{17}{20}x=3417$
$\Rightarrow x=\left(\frac{3417\times20}{17}\right)=4020$.

Let $40\%$ of $265+35\%$ of $180=50\%$ of $x$.
Then, $\left(\frac{40}{100}\times265\right)+\left(\frac{35}{100}\times180\right)=\left(\frac{50}{100}\times x\right)$
$\Rightarrow 106+63=\frac{x}{2}$
$\Rightarrow x=169\times2=338$.

Let $x\%$ of $450+46\%$ of $285=257.1$. Then,
$\left(\frac{x}{100}\times450\right)+\left(\frac{46}{100}\times285\right)=257.1$
$\Rightarrow \frac{9}{2}x=257.1-131.1=126$
$\Rightarrow x=\frac{126\times2}{9}=28$.

Let $35568\div x\%$ of $650=456$. Then,
$35568\div\left(\frac{x}{100}\times650\right)=456$
$\Rightarrow \frac{13}{2}x\times456=35568$
$\Rightarrow 2964x=35568$
$\Rightarrow x=\frac{35568}{2964}=12$.

Let $23\%$ of $8040+42\%$ of $545=x\%$ of $3000$. Then,
$\left(\frac{23}{100}\times8040\right)+\left(\frac{42}{100}\times545\right)=\left(\frac{x}{100}\times3000\right)$
$\Rightarrow 30x=1849.2+228.9=2078.1$
$\Rightarrow x=\frac{2078.1}{30}=69.27$.

Let $3.2\%$ of $500\times2.4\%$ of $x=288$.
Then, $\left(\frac{32}{10}\times\frac{1}{100}\times500\right)\times\left(\frac{24}{10}\times\frac{1}{100}\times x\right)=288$
$\Rightarrow 16\times\frac{3}{125}x=288$
$\Rightarrow x=\frac{288\times125}{16\times3}=750$.

Let $40\%$ of $4.5+x\%$ of $\frac{2}{3}=20\%$ of $10$.
Then, $\left(\frac{40}{100}\times4.5\right)+\left(\frac{x}{100}\times\frac{2}{3}\right)=\left(\frac{20}{100}\times10\right)$
$\Rightarrow \frac{x}{150}=2-1.8=0.2$
$\Rightarrow x=0.2\times150=30$.

questions.question_text: 30% of 28% of 480 is the same as
questions.solution:
Clearly, $60\%$ of $28\%$ of $240=\left(\frac{60}{100}\times\frac{28}{100}\times240\right)$
$=\left(\frac{30}{100}\times\frac{28}{100}\times480\right)$
$=30\%$ of $285\%$ of $480$.

questions.question_text: What is 25% of 25% equal to?
What percent is 3% of 5%?
questions.solution:
Required percentage $=\left(\frac{3\%}{5\%}\right)\times100\%$
$=\left[\frac{(3/100)}{(5/100)}\right]\times100\%=60\%$.

questions.question_text: 64% of a number is 2592. What is 88% of that number?
questions.solution:
Let the number be $x$.
Then, $64\%$ of $x=2592$
$\Rightarrow \frac{64}{100}x=2592$
$\Rightarrow x=\left(\frac{2592\times100}{64}\right)=4050$.

questions.question_text: 42% of a number is 892.5. What is 73% of that number?
questions.solution:
Let the number be $x$.
Then, $42\%$ of $x=892.50$
$\Rightarrow \frac{42}{100}x=892.50$
$\Rightarrow x=\left(\frac{892.5\times100}{42}\right)=2125$.
$\therefore 73\%$ of $2125=\left(\frac{73}{100}\times2125\right)=1551.25$.

questions.question_text: 15% of 45% of a number is 105.3. What is 24% of that number?
questions.solution:
Let the number be $x$. Then, $15\%$ of $45\%$ of $x=105.3$
$\Rightarrow \left(\frac{15}{100}\times\frac{45}{100}\times x\right)=105.3$
$\Rightarrow \frac{27}{400}x=105.3$
$\Rightarrow x=\left(\frac{105.3\times400}{27}\right)=1560$.
$\therefore 24\%$ of $1560=\left(\frac{24}{100}\times1560\right)=374.4$.

questions.question_text: If 0.03 is X% of 0.3, then the value of X is
questions.solution:
$X\%$ of $0.3=0.03$
$\Rightarrow \frac{X}{100}\times0.3=0.03$
$\Rightarrow X=\left(\frac{0.03\times100}{0.3}\right)=10$.

questions.question_text: 40% of 60% of 32% of an amount is ₹ 432. What is the amount?
questions.solution:
Let the required amount be ₹ $x$.
Then, $40\%$ of $60\%$ of $32\%$ of $x=432$
$\Rightarrow \frac{40}{100}\times\frac{60}{100}\times\frac{32}{100}\times x=432$
$\Rightarrow x=\left(\frac{432\times100\times100\times100}{40\times60\times32}\right)=5625$.

questions.question_text: Find the greatest possible number for which 30% of that number is less than 100.
questions.solution:
Let the required number be $x$.
Then, $30\%$ of $x<100$
$\Rightarrow \frac{30}{100}x<100$
$\Rightarrow x<\frac{1000}{3}$
$\Rightarrow x<333.33$.
Hence, greatest possible value of $x=333$.

questions.question_text: If ₹ 2800 is $\frac{2}{7}$ percent of the value of a house, the worth of the house (in ₹) is
questions.solution:
Let the worth of the house be ₹ $x$.
Then, $\frac{2}{7}\%$ of $x=28000$
$\Leftrightarrow \frac{2}{7\times100}\times x=28000$
$\Leftrightarrow x=\left(\frac{28000\times100\times7}{2}\right)=9,80,000$.

Let $15\%$ of $x\%$ of $582 = 17.46$.
Then, $\frac{15}{100}\times\frac{x}{100}\times582 = 17.46$
$\Leftrightarrow x = \frac{1746}{100}\times\frac{100}{100}\times\frac{100}{15\times582}$
$= 20$.

questions.question_text: In a year, a man manages to sell only 65% of the chicken he owns. How many chicken should the man own to sell 47775 chicken in a year?
questions.solution:
Let the required number of chicken be $x$.
Then, $65\%$ of $x = 47775$
$\Rightarrow \frac{65}{100}x = 47775$
$\Rightarrow x = \frac{47775\times100}{65} = 73500$.

questions.question_text: If 35% of a number is 175, then what percent of 175 is that number?
questions.solution:
Let the number be $x$.
Then, $35\%$ of $x = 175$
$\Leftrightarrow \frac{35}{100}\times x = 175 \Leftrightarrow x = \frac{175\times100}{35} = 500$.
Now, let $y\%$ of $175 = 500$.
Then, $\frac{y}{100}\times175 = 500 \Leftrightarrow y = \frac{500\times100}{175} = \frac{2000}{7} = 285\frac{5}{7}$.

questions.question_text: If an electricity bill is paid before due date, one gets a reduction of 4% on the amount of the bill. By paying the bill before due date a person got a reduction of ₹ 13. The amount of his electricity bill was
questions.solution:
Let the amount of the bill be ₹ $x$.
Then, $4\%$ of $x = 13 \Rightarrow \frac{4}{100}\times x = 13 \Rightarrow x = \frac{13\times100}{4} = 325$.

questions.question_text: In an examination X secures 58% marks and Y secures 105 marks less than X. If the maximum marks were 700, then what percent of marks did Y secure?
questions.solution:
Marks secured by $X = 58\%$ of $700 = \frac{58}{100}\times700 = 406$.
Marks secured by $Y = (406 - 105) = 301$.
$\therefore$ Required percentage = $\frac{301}{700}\times100\% = 43\%$.

questions.question_text: Ms. Pooja invests 13% of her monthly salary, i.e. ₹ 8554 in Mediclaim Policies. Later she invests 23% of her monthly salary on Child Education Policies ; also she invests another 8% of her monthly salary on Mutual Funds. What is the total annual amount invested by Ms. Pooja?
questions.solution:
Let Pooja’s monthly salary be ₹ $x$.
Then, $13\%$ of $x = 8554 \Rightarrow \frac{13}{100}x = 8554$
$\Rightarrow x = \frac{8554\times100}{13} = 65800$.
Total percentage of salary invested = $(13 + 23 + 8)\% = 44\%$.
$\therefore$ Total amount invested monthly = $44\%$ of ₹ $65800$
$= \frac{44}{100}\times65800 = 28952$.

questions.question_text: Nupur invests ₹ 89856, which is 26% of her annual income, in mutual funds. What is her monthly income?
questions.solution:
Let Nupur’s annual salary be ₹ $x$.
Then, $26\%$ of $x = 89856 \Rightarrow \frac{26}{100}x = 89856$
$\Rightarrow x = \frac{89856\times100}{26} = 345600$.
$\therefore$ Nupur’s monthly income = ₹ $\frac{345600}{12} = 28800$.

questions.question_text: David and his wife each receives an 8 percent annual rise. If David receives a raise of ₹ 800 and his wife receives a raise of ₹ 840, what is the difference between their annual incomes after their raises?
questions.solution:
Let David’s annual income be ₹ $x$ and his wife’s annual income be ₹ $y$.
Then, $8\%$ of $x = 800 \Rightarrow \frac{8}{100}x = 800$
$\Rightarrow x = \frac{800\times100}{8} = 10000$.
And, $8\%$ of $y = 840 \Rightarrow \frac{8}{100}y = 840$
$\Rightarrow y = \frac{840\times100}{8} = 10500$.
$\therefore$ Required difference = ₹ $[(10500 + 840) - (10000 + 800)]$
$= ₹ (11340 - 10800) = 540$.

questions.question_text: If the average of a number, its 75% and its 25% is 240, then the number is
questions.solution:
Let the number be $x$.
Then, $x + \frac{75\%\text{ of }x + 25\%\text{ of }x}{3} = 240$
$\Rightarrow x + \frac{\frac{75}{100}x + \frac{25}{100}x}{3} = 240 \times 3 = 720$
$\Rightarrow x + \frac{\frac{3}{4}x + \frac{1}{4}x}{3} = 720$
$\Rightarrow 2x = 720 \Rightarrow x = 360$.

questions.question_text: A company bought a total of 60 computers and 20 printers to modernise billing operations. If the price of each computer was three times the price of each printer, what percent of the total cost of the purchase was the total cost of the printers?
questions.solution:
Let the price of each printer be ₹ $x$. Then, price of each computer = ₹ $(3x)$.
Total cost of printers = ₹ $(20x)$.
Total cost of purchase = ₹ $(60 \times 3x + 20x) = ₹ (200x)$.
$\therefore$ Required percentage = $\left(\frac{20x}{200x}\times100\right)\% = 10\%$.

questions.question_text: An individual pays 30% income tax. On this tax he has to pay a surcharge of 10%. Thus the net tax rate, he has to pay, is
questions.solution:
Let his taxable income be ₹ $100$.
Then, income tax = $30\%$ of ₹ $100 = 30$.
Surcharge = $10\%$ of ₹ $30 = 3$.
Total tax paid = ₹ $(30 + 3) = 33$.
$\therefore$ Net tax rate = $\frac{33}{100}\times100\% = 33\%$.

questions.question_text: Anand has drawn an angle of measure 45° 27′ when he was asked to draw an angle of 45°. The percentage error in his drawing is
questions.solution:
Error = $(45^{\circ}27' - 45^{\circ}) = 27'$.
Accurate measure = $45^{\circ} = (45 \times 60)' = 2700'$.
$\therefore$ Percentage error = $\left(\frac{27}{2700}\times100\right)\% = 1\%$.

questions.question_text: The value of which of the following fractions is less than twenty percent?
The difference between 54% of a number and 26% of the same number is 22526. What is 66% of that number?
questions.solution:
Let the number be $x$.
Then, $54\%$ of $x - 26\%$ of $x = 22526$
$\Rightarrow \frac{54-26}{100}x = 22526 \Rightarrow \frac{28}{100}x = 22526$
$\Rightarrow x = \left(\frac{22526\times100}{28}\right) = 80450$.
$\therefore 66\%$ of $80450 = \frac{66}{100}\times80450 = 53097$.

questions.question_text: The difference between 38% of a number and 24% of the same number is ₹ 135.10. What is 40% of that number?
questions.solution:
Let the number be $x$.
Then, $38\%$ of $x - 24\%$ of $x = 135.10$
$\Rightarrow \frac{38-24}{100}x = 135.10 \Rightarrow \frac{14}{100}x = 135.10$
$\Rightarrow x = \left(\frac{135.10\times100}{14}\right) = 965$.
$\therefore 40\%$ of $965 = \frac{40}{100}\times965 = 386$.

questions.question_text: 76% of the students in a school are boys. If the number of girls is 204, then the total number of students is
questions.solution:
Let the total number of students be $x$.
Then, $(100 - 76)\%$ of $x = 204$
$\Rightarrow 24\%$ of $x = 204$
$\Rightarrow \frac{24}{100}x = 204 \Rightarrow x = \left(\frac{204\times100}{24}\right) = 850$.

questions.question_text: In an examination, 65% of the total examinees passed. If the number of failures is 420, the total number of examinees is
questions.solution:
Let the total number of examinees be $x$.
Then, $(100 - 65)\%$ of $x = 420 \Rightarrow 35\%$ of $x = 420$
$\Rightarrow \frac{35}{100}x = 420 \Rightarrow x = \left(\frac{420\times100}{35}\right) = 1200$.

questions.question_text: There are 340 vacancies for a particular post in an organisation. Experience shows that 15% of the candidates interviewed get rejected. How many candidates should be interviewed to fill all the vacancies?
questions.solution:
Let the total number of candidates be $x$.
Then, $(100 - 15)\%$ of $x = 340 \Rightarrow 85\%$ of $x = 340$
$\Rightarrow \frac{85}{100}x = 340 \Rightarrow x = \left(\frac{340\times100}{85}\right) = 400$.

questions.question_text: Rajan got 76 percent marks and Sonia got 480 marks in a test. The maximum marks of the test is equal to the marks obtained by Rajan and Sonia together. How many marks did Rajan score in the test?
questions.solution:
Let the maximum marks be $x$.
Then, $76\%$ of $x + 480 = x \Rightarrow x - 76\%$ of $x = 480$
$\Rightarrow 24\%$ of $x = 480$
$\Rightarrow \frac{24}{100}x = 480 \Rightarrow x = \left(\frac{480\times100}{24}\right) = 2000$.
$\therefore$ Marks scored by Rajan = $76\%$ of $2000 = \frac{76}{100}\times2000 = 1520$.

questions.question_text: If a bucket is 80% full, then it contains 2 litres more water than when it is $66\frac{2}{3}$% full. What is the capacity of the bucket?
questions.solution:
Let the capacity of the bucket be $x$ litres.
Then, $80\%$ of $x - 66\frac{2}{3}\%$ of $x = 2$
$\Rightarrow \left(80 - 66\frac{2}{3}\right)\%$ of $x = 2 \Rightarrow 13\frac{1}{3}\%$ of $x = 2$
$\Rightarrow \frac{40}{3\times100}\times x = 2 \Rightarrow x = \left(\frac{2\times100\times3}{40}\right) = 15$.

questions.question_text: Vinay decided to donate 5% of his salary. On the day of donation he changed his mind and donated ₹ 1687.50, which was 75% of what he had decided earlier. How much is Vinay's salary?
questions.solution:
Let Vinay's salary be ₹ $x$. Then,
$75\%$ of $5\%$ of $x = 1687.50$
$\Rightarrow \frac{75}{100} \times \frac{5}{100} \times x = 1687.50$
$\Rightarrow x = \left(\frac{1687.50 \times 100 \times 100}{75 \times 5}\right) = 45000$.

questions.question_text: One-fourth of sixty percent of a number is equal to two-fifths of twenty percent of another number. What is the respective ratio of the first number to the second number?
questions.solution:
Let the first number be $x$ and the second number be $y$. Then,
$\frac{1}{4}$ of $60\%$ of $x = \frac{2}{5}$ of $20\%$ of $y$
$\Rightarrow \frac{1}{4} \times \frac{60}{100} \times x = \frac{2}{5} \times \frac{20}{100} \times y \Rightarrow \frac{3}{20}x = \frac{2}{25}y$
$\Rightarrow \frac{x}{y} = \frac{2}{25} \times \frac{20}{3} = \frac{8}{15}$.

questions.question_text: The sum of two numbers is 2490. If 6.5% of one number is equal to 8.5% of the other, the greater number is
questions.solution:
Let the numbers be $x$ and $(2490 - x)$. Then,
$6.5\%$ of $x = 8.5\%$ of $(2490 - x)$
$\Rightarrow \frac{65}{10} \times \frac{1}{100} \times x = \frac{85}{10} \times \frac{1}{100} \times (2490 - x)$
$\Rightarrow 13x = 17(2490 - x) \Rightarrow 30x = 2490 \times 17$
$\Rightarrow x = \left(\frac{2490 \times 17}{30}\right) = 1411$.
Hence, the numbers are $1411$ and $1079$.

questions.question_text: The number of students who opted for IT courses decreased by 23%. If the number is 1540 now, then the original number of students opting for IT courses was
questions.solution:
Then, $(100 - 23)\%$ of $x = 1540$
$\Rightarrow 77\%$ of $x = 1540$
$\Rightarrow \frac{77}{100} \times x = 1540 \Rightarrow x = \left(\frac{1540 \times 100}{77}\right) = 2000$.

questions.question_text: If the monthly salary of an employee is increased by $2\frac{2}{3}$%, he gets ₹ 72 more. His monthly salary (in ₹) is
questions.solution:
Let the monthly salary be ₹ $x$.
Then, $2\frac{2}{3}\%$ of $x = 72$
$\Rightarrow \frac{8}{3 \times 100} \times x = 72 \Rightarrow x = \left(\frac{72 \times 3 \times 100}{8}\right) = 2700$.

questions.question_text: A store raised the price of an item by exactly 10 percent. Which of the following could not be the resulting price of the item?
questions.solution:
Let the original price be ₹ $x$ and increased price be ₹ $y$.
Then, $110\%$ of $x = y \Rightarrow x = \frac{10}{11}y$.
(a) Original price = ₹ $\left(\frac{10}{11} \times 5.50\right) = 5$.
(b) Original price = ₹ $\left(\frac{10}{11} \times 7.60\right) = 6.9090 \dots$
which is not possible.
(c) Original price = ₹ $\left(\frac{10}{11} \times 11\right) = 10$.
(d) Original price = ₹ $\left(\frac{10}{11} \times 12.10\right) = 11$.

questions.question_text: A number increased by $1\frac{37}{2}$% gives 33. The number is
questions.solution:
Let the number be $x$.
Then, $\left(100 + 37\frac{1}{2}\right)\%$ of $x = 33$
$\Rightarrow 137\frac{1}{2}\%$ of $x = 33 \Rightarrow \frac{275}{2 \times 100} \times x = 33$
$\Rightarrow x = \left(\frac{33 \times 2 \times 100}{275}\right) = 24$.

questions.question_text: Three-fourths of a number is equal to 60% of another number and the difference between these two numbers is 20. What is the sum of these two numbers?
questions.solution:
Let the numbers be $x$ and $x + 20$. Then,
$\frac{3}{4}x = 60\%$ of $(x + 20)$
$\Rightarrow \frac{3}{4}x = \frac{60}{100}(x + 20) \Rightarrow \frac{5}{4}x = x + 20$
$\Rightarrow \frac{x}{4} = 20 \Rightarrow x = 20 \times 4 = 80$.
$\therefore$ Required sum = $x + x + 20 = 2x + 20 = 2 \times 80 + 20 = 180$.

questions.question_text: The number which exceeds 16% of it by 42 is
questions.solution:
Let the number be $x$. Then, $x - 16\%$ of $x = 42$
$\Leftrightarrow x - \frac{16}{100}x = 42 \Leftrightarrow x - \frac{4}{25}x = 42$
$\Leftrightarrow \frac{21}{25}x = 42 \Leftrightarrow x = \left(\frac{42 \times 25}{21}\right) = 50$.

questions.question_text: What percentage of numbers from 1 to 70 have squares that end in the digit 1?
questions.solution:
Clearly, the numbers which have $1$ or $9$ in the unit's digit, have squares that end in the digit $1$. Such numbers from $1$ to $70$ are $1, 9, 11, 19, 21, 29, 31, 39, 41, 49, 51, 59, 61, 69$.
Number of such numbers = $14$.
$\therefore$ Required percentage = $\left(\frac{14}{70} \times 100\right)\% = 20\%$.

questions.question_text: By how much percent is four-fifths of 70 lesser than five-sevenths of 112?
questions.solution:
$\frac{4}{5} \times 70 = 56$ and $\frac{5}{7} \times 112 = 80$.
$\therefore$ Required percentage = $\left(\frac{80 - 56}{80} \times 100\right)\%$
$= \left(\frac{24}{80} \times 100\right)\% = 30\%$.

questions.question_text: If a number x is 10% less than another number y and y is 10% more than 125, then x is equal to
If 75% of a number is added to 75, then the result is the number itself. The number is
questions.solution:
Let the number be $x$. Then,
$75\%$ of $x + 75 = x \Leftrightarrow x - \frac{75}{100}x = 75 \Leftrightarrow x - \frac{3}{4}x = 75$
$\Leftrightarrow \frac{x}{4} = 75 \Leftrightarrow x = 300$.

questions.question_text: When 125 is subtracted from a number, it reduces to its 37.5 percent. What is 25 percent of that number?
questions.solution:
Let the number be $x$.
Then, $x - 125 = 37\frac{1}{2}\%$ of $x \Rightarrow x - 125 = \frac{75}{2 \times 100}x = \frac{3}{8}x$
$\Rightarrow x - \frac{3}{8}x = 125 \Rightarrow \frac{5}{8}x = 125$
$\Rightarrow x = \left(\frac{125 \times 8}{5}\right) = 200$.
$\therefore 25\%$ of $200 = \left(\frac{25}{100} \times 200\right) = 50$.

questions.question_text: Which of the following multipliers will cause a number to be increased by 29.7%?
questions.solution:
Let the number be $100$ and required multiplier be $y$.
Then, $100y = 129.7$ or $y = \frac{129.7}{100} = 1.297$.

questions.question_text: The sum of two numbers is $\frac{28}{25}$ of the first number. The second number is what percent of the first?
questions.solution:
Let the numbers be $x$ and $y$. Then,
$x + y = \frac{28}{25}x \Leftrightarrow y = \frac{28}{25}x - x$
$\Leftrightarrow y = \frac{3}{25}x \Leftrightarrow \frac{y}{x} = \left(\frac{3}{25} \times 100\right)\% = 12\%$.

questions.question_text: A number reduced by 25% becomes 225. What percent should it be increased so that it becomes 390?
questions.solution:
Let the number be $x$.
Then, $x - 25\%$ of $x = 225 \Rightarrow x - \frac{25}{100}x = 225 \Rightarrow \frac{75}{100}x = 225$
$\Rightarrow x = \left(\frac{225 \times 100}{75}\right) = 300$.
Required increase = $(390 - 300) = 90$.
$\therefore$ Increase $\% = \left(\frac{90}{300} \times 100\right)\% = 30\%$.

questions.question_text: If 25% of a number is subtracted from a second number, the second number reduces to its five-sixths. What is the ratio of the first number to the second number?
questions.solution:
Let the numbers be $x$ and $y$.
Then, $y - 25\%$ of $x = \frac{5}{6}y \Leftrightarrow y - \frac{5}{6}y = \frac{25}{100}x \Leftrightarrow \frac{1}{6}y = \frac{1}{4}x \Leftrightarrow \frac{x}{y} = \frac{4}{6} = \frac{2}{3}$.

questions.question_text: The difference of two numbers is 20% of the larger number. If the smaller number is 20, then the larger number is:
questions.solution:
Let the larger number be $x$.
Then, $x - 20 = \frac{20}{100}x \Leftrightarrow x - \frac{1}{5}x = 20$
$\Leftrightarrow \frac{4}{5}x = 20 \Leftrightarrow x = \left(20 \times \frac{5}{4}\right) = 25$.

questions.question_text: When any number is divided by 12, then dividend becomes $\frac{1}{4}$th of the other number. By how much percent first number is greater than the second number?
questions.solution:
Let the numbers be $x$ and $y$. Then, $\frac{x}{12} = \frac{y}{4} \Leftrightarrow x = 3y$.
$\therefore$ Required percentage = $\left(\frac{x - y}{y} \times 100\right)\%$
$= \left(\frac{2y}{y} \times 100\right)\% = 200\%$.

questions.question_text: If one number is 80% of the other and 4 times the sum of their squares is 656, then the numbers are
questions.solution:
Let one number = $x$. Then, other number = 80% of $x = \frac{4}{5}x$.
$\left[x^2 + \left(\frac{4}{5}x\right)^2\right] = 656 \Leftrightarrow x^2 + \frac{16}{25}x^2 = 164 \Leftrightarrow \frac{41}{25}x^2 = 164$
$\Leftrightarrow x^2 = \left(\frac{164 \times 25}{41}\right) = 100 \Leftrightarrow x = 10$.
So, the numbers are 10 and 8.

questions.question_text: Two numbers A and B are such that the sum of 5% of A and 4% of B is two-thirds of the sum of 6% of A and 8% of B. Find the ratio of A:B.
questions.solution:
5% of $A + 4\%$ of $B = \frac{2}{3} (6\%$ of $A + 8\%$ of $B)$
$\Leftrightarrow \frac{5}{100}A + \frac{4}{100}B = \frac{2}{3} \left(\frac{6}{100}A + \frac{8}{100}B\right)$
$\Leftrightarrow \frac{1}{20}A + \frac{1}{25}B = \frac{1}{25}A + \frac{4}{75}B$
$\Leftrightarrow \left(\frac{1}{20} - \frac{1}{25}\right)A = \left(\frac{4}{75} - \frac{1}{25}\right)B$
$\Leftrightarrow \frac{1}{100}A = \frac{1}{75}B \Leftrightarrow \frac{A}{B} = \frac{100}{75} = \frac{4}{3}$.

questions.question_text: Three candidates contested an election and received 1136, 7636 and 11628 votes respectively. What percentage of the total votes did the winning candidate get?
questions.solution:
Total number of votes polled = $(1136 + 7636 + 11628) = 20400$.
$\therefore$ Required percentage = $\left(\frac{11628}{20400} \times 100\right) \% = 57\%$.

questions.question_text: The population of a town increased from 1,75,000 to 2,62,500 in a decade. The average percent increase of population per year is
questions.solution:
Increase in 10 years = $(262500 - 175000) = 87500$.
Increase% = $\left(\frac{87500}{175000} \times 100\right) \% = 50\%$.
$\therefore$ Required average = $\left(\frac{50}{10}\right) \% = 5\%$.

questions.question_text: A student multiplied a number by $\frac{3}{5}$ instead of $\frac{5}{3}$. What is the percentage error in the calculation?
questions.solution:
Let the number be $x$. Then, error = $\frac{5}{3}x - \frac{3}{5}x = \frac{16}{15}x$.
Error % = $\left(\frac{16x/15}{5x/3} \times 100\right) \% = 64\%$.

questions.question_text: A tempo is insured to the extent of $\frac{4}{5}$ of its original value. If the premium on it at the rate of 1.3 percent amounts to ₹ 910, the original value of the tempo is
questions.solution:
Let the original value of the tempo be ₹ $x$. Then,
1.3% of $\frac{4}{5}$ of $x = 910 \Leftrightarrow \frac{13}{10} \times \frac{1}{100} \times \frac{4}{5} \times x = 910$
$\Leftrightarrow x = \left(\frac{910 \times 10 \times 100 \times 5}{13 \times 4}\right) = 87500$.

questions.question_text: When 15% is lost in grinding wheat, a country can export 30 lakh tons of wheat. On the other hand, if 10% is lost in grinding, it can export 40 lakh tons of wheat. The production of wheat in the country is:
questions.solution:
Let the total production be $x$ lakh tons. Then, 15% of $x - 10\%$ of $x = (40 - 30)$ lakh tons
$\Leftrightarrow 5\%$ of $x = 10$ lakh tons
$\Leftrightarrow x = \left(\frac{10 \times 100}{5}\right) = 200$ lakh tons.

questions.question_text: In a competitive examination in State A, 6% candidates got selected from the total appeared candidates. State B had an equal number of candidates appeared and 7% candidates got selected with 80 more candidates got selected than A. What was the number of candidates appeared from each State?
questions.solution:
Let the number of candidates appeared from each state be $x$.
Then, 7% of $x - 6\%$ of $x = 80$
$\Leftrightarrow 1\%$ of $x = 80$
$\Leftrightarrow x = 80 \times 100 = 8000$.

questions.question_text: The price of a car is ₹ 3,25,000. It was insured to 85% of its price. The car was damaged completely in an accident and the insurance company paid 90% of the insurance. What was the difference between the price of the car and the amount received?
questions.solution:
Amount paid to car owner = 90% of 85% of ₹ 3,25,000
= ₹ $\left(\frac{90}{100} \times \frac{85}{100} \times 325000\right) = 2,48,625$.
$\therefore$ Required difference = ₹ $(325000 - 248625) = 76,375$.

questions.question_text: Gauri went to the stationers and bought things worth ₹ 25, out of which 30 paise went on sales tax on taxable purchases. If the tax rate was 6%, then what was the cost of the tax free items?
questions.solution:
Let the amount of taxable purchases be ₹ $x$.
Then, 6% of $x = 30 \Leftrightarrow x = \left(\frac{30 \times 100}{6}\right) = 500$.
Cost of tax free items = ₹ $[25 - (5 + 0.30)] = 19.70$.

questions.question_text: A batsman scored 110 runs which included 3 boundaries and 8 sixes. What percent of his total score did he make by running between the wickets?
questions.solution:
Number of runs made by running
$= 110 - (3 \times 4 + 8 \times 6) = 50$.
$\therefore$ Required percentage = $\left(\frac{50}{110} \times 100\right) \% = 45\frac{5}{11}\%$.

questions.question_text: After deducting a commission of 5%, a T.V. set costs ₹ 9595. Its marked price is:
questions.solution:
Let the marked price be ₹ $x$.
Then, $x - 5\%$ of $x = 9595 \Leftrightarrow 95\%$ of $x = 9595$
$\Leftrightarrow x = \left(\frac{9595 \times 100}{95}\right) = 10100$.

questions.question_text: A person who spends $66\frac{2}{3}$% of his income is able to save ₹ 1200 per month. His monthly expenses (in ₹) are
questions.solution:
Let the monthly income be ₹ $x$. Then,
$\left(100 - 66\frac{2}{3}\right)\%$ of $x = 1200$
$\Leftrightarrow 33\frac{1}{3}\%$ of $x = 1200$
$\Leftrightarrow \left(\frac{100}{3} \times \frac{1}{100}\right) \times x = 1200$
$\Leftrightarrow x = 1200 \times 3 = 3600$.
$\therefore$ Monthly expenses = ₹ $(3600 - 1200) = 2400$.

questions.question_text: Twenty-five percent of Reena’s yearly income is equal to seventy-five percent of Anubhav‘s monthly income. If Anubhav‘s yearly income is ₹ 240000, what is Reena's monthly income?
questions.solution:
Let Reena's yearly income be ₹ $x$.
Anubhav's monthly income = ₹ $\left(\frac{240000}{12}\right) = 20000$.
Then, 25% of $x = 75\%$ of 20000
$\Rightarrow \frac{25}{100}x = \frac{75}{100} \times 20000$
$\Rightarrow \frac{x}{4} = 15000 \Rightarrow x = 60000$.
$\therefore$ Reena’s monthly income = ₹ $\left(\frac{60000}{12}\right) = 5000$.

questions.question_text: Twelve percent of Kaushal‘s monthly salary is equal to sixteen percent of Nandini’s monthly salary. Sonal’s monthly salary is half that of Nandini’s. If Sonal’s annual salary is ₹ 1.08 lacs, what is Kaushal’s monthly salary?
questions.solution:
Sonal's annual salary = ₹ 108000. Sonal’s monthly salary
= ₹ $\left(\frac{108000}{12}\right) = 9000$.
Nandini’s monthly salary = ₹ $(9000 \times 2) = 18000$.
Let Kaushal’s monthly salary be ₹ $x$. Then,
12% of $x = 16\%$ of 18000
$\Rightarrow \frac{12}{100}x = \frac{16}{100} \times 18000 = 2880$
$\Rightarrow x = \left(\frac{2880 \times 100}{12}\right) = 24000$.

questions.question_text: Aman’s expense is 30% more than Vimal’s and Vimal’s expense is 10% less than Raman’s. If the sum of their expenses is ₹ 6447, then what would be Aman’s expense?
questions.solution:
Let Raman’s expense be ₹ $x$. Then, Vimal’s expense = 90% of ₹ $x = ₹ \left(\frac{90}{100} \times x\right) = ₹ \frac{9}{10}x$.
Aman’s expense = 130% of ₹ $\frac{9}{10}x = \left(\frac{130}{100} \times \frac{9}{10}x\right) = \frac{117}{100}x$.
$\therefore \frac{117}{100}x + \frac{9}{10}x + x = 6447 \Rightarrow \frac{117x + 90x + 100x}{100} = 6447$
$\Rightarrow 307x = 644700$
$\Rightarrow x = \frac{644700}{307} = 2100$.
Hence, Aman’s expense = ₹ $\left(\frac{117 \times 2100}{100}\right) = 2457$.

questions.question_text: Two tailors X and Y are paid a total of ₹ 550 per week by their employer. If X is paid 20 percent more than the sum paid to Y, how much is Y paid per week?
questions.solution:
Let the sum paid to Y per week be ₹ $x$.
Then, sum paid to X per week
$= 120\%$ of ₹ $x = ₹ \left(\frac{120}{100} \times x\right) = ₹ \frac{6}{5}x$.
$\therefore x + \frac{6}{5}x = 550 \Rightarrow \frac{11x}{5} = 550 \Rightarrow x = \frac{550 \times 5}{11} = 250$.

questions.question_text: Prithvi spent ₹ 89745 on his college fees, ₹ 51291 on Personality Development Classes and the remaining 27% of the total amount he had as cash with him. What was the total amount?
questions.solution:
Let the total amount be ₹ $x$. Then, $(100 - 27)\%$ of $x = 89745 + 51291$
$\Rightarrow 73\%$ of $x = 141036$
$\Rightarrow \frac{73}{100}x = 141036$
$\Rightarrow x = \left(\frac{141036 \times 100}{73}\right) = 193200$.

questions.question_text: If a number is reduced by 40% it becomes two-thirds of another number. What is the ratio of the first number to the second number?
questions.solution:
Let the numbers be $x$ and $y$ respectively.
Then, 60% of $x = \frac{2}{3}y \Rightarrow \frac{60}{100}x = \frac{2}{3}y \Rightarrow \frac{3}{5}x = \frac{2}{3}y$
$\Rightarrow \frac{x}{y} = \frac{2}{3} \times \frac{5}{3} = \frac{10}{9}$.

questions.question_text: To meet a government requirement, a bottler must test 5 percent of its spring water and 10 percent of its sparkling water for purity. If a customer ordered 120 cases of spring water and 80 cases of sparkling water, then what percent of all the cases must the bottler test before he can send it out?
questions.solution:
Number of cases required to be tested
$= 5\%$ of $120 + 10\%$ of $80$
$= \left(\frac{5}{100} \times 120\right) + \left(\frac{10}{100} \times 80\right) = 6 + 8 = 14$.
$\therefore$ Required percentage
$= \left(\frac{14}{120 + 80} \times 100\right) \% = \left(\frac{14}{200} \times 100\right) \% = 7\%$.

questions.question_text: When income tax is 3 paise in a rupee, a person's net income is ₹ 237650. What will it be when the income tax is raised to 7 paise?
questions.solution:
Let the person's gross income be ₹ $x$. Then, $(100-3)\%$ of $x = 237650 \Rightarrow 97\%$ of $x = 237650$
$\Rightarrow \frac{97}{100}x = 237650$
$\Rightarrow x = \left(\frac{237650 \times 100}{97}\right) = 245000$.
When income tax is raised to $7\%$, we have:
Net income
$= (100-7)\%$ of ₹ $245000 = 93\%$ of ₹ $245000$
$= ₹ \left(\frac{93}{100} \times 245000\right) = ₹ 227850$.

questions.question_text: A monthly return railway ticket costs 25 percent more than a single ticket. A week’s extension can be had for the former by paying 5 percent of the monthly ticket’s cost. If the money paid for the monthly ticket (with extension) is ₹ 84, the price of the single ticket is
questions.solution:
Let the cost of a single ticket be ₹ $x$.
Then, cost of monthly return ticket
$= 125\%$ of ₹ $x = ₹ \left(\frac{125}{100}x\right) = ₹ \frac{5x}{4}$.
Cost of monthly return ticket with extension
$= 105\%$ of ₹ $\frac{5x}{4} = ₹ \left(\frac{105}{100} \times \frac{5x}{4}\right) = ₹ \frac{21x}{16}$.
$\frac{21x}{16} = 84 \Rightarrow x = \left(\frac{84 \times 16}{21}\right) = 64$.

questions.question_text: In limestone, 40% is calcium and the rest is carbon and oxygen. If in 20 kg of limestone, there is 9.4 kg of oxygen, then what is the percentage of carbon in it?
questions.solution:
Percentage of oxygen $= \left(\frac{9.4}{20} \times 100\right)\% = 47\%$.
Percentage of carbon $= [100 - (40 + 47)]\% = 13\%$.

questions.question_text: In a class of 72 children, children are seated in rows and columns in such a way that the number of children in each row is 12.5% more than the number of children in each column. How many children are there in each row?
questions.solution:
Let the number of children in each column be $x$.
Then, number of children in each row $= 112\frac{1}{2}\%$ of $x$
$= \frac{225}{2} \times \frac{1}{100} \times x = \frac{9x}{8}$.
$x \times \frac{9x}{8} = 72 \Rightarrow x^2 = \frac{72 \times 8}{9} = 64 \Rightarrow x = 8$.
So, number of children in each row $= \frac{9}{8}x = 9$.

questions.question_text: The owner of a boutique decides to calculate the percentage of customers who purchase hats. If 40 percent of the store’s customers decide to purchase items, and of those customers 15 percent purchase hats, what percent of the store’s customers purchase hats?
questions.solution:
Let the total number of customers be $x$.
Then, number of customers who purchase hats $= 15\%$ of
$40\%$ of $x = \left(\frac{15}{100} \times \frac{40}{100} \times x\right) = \frac{6x}{100}$
$= 6\%$ of $x$.
Required percentage $= 6\%$.

questions.question_text: In a market research project, 20% opted for Nirma detergent whereas 60% opted for Surf Blue detergent. The remaining individuals were not certain. If the difference between those who opted for Surf Blue and those who were uncertain was 720, how many respondents were covered in the survey?
questions.solution:
Let the total number of respondents be $x$.
Percentage of uncertain individuals
$= [100 - (20 + 60)]\% = 20\%$.
$60\%$ of $x - 20\%$ of $x = 720 \Rightarrow 40\%$ of $x = 720$
$\Rightarrow \frac{40}{100}x = 720 \Rightarrow x = \left(\frac{720 \times 100}{40}\right) = 1800$.

questions.question_text: In an examination it is required to get 36% of the aggregate marks to pass. A student gets 198 marks and is declared failed by 36 marks. What is the maximum aggregate marks a student can get?
questions.solution:
Let the maximum marks be $x$.
Then, $36\%$ of $x = 198 + 36 \Rightarrow \frac{36x}{100} = 234 \Rightarrow x = \left(\frac{234 \times 100}{36}\right)$
$= 650$.

questions.question_text: In a test, minimum passing percentage for girls and boys is 35% and 40% respectively. A boy scored 483 marks and failed by 117 marks. What is the minimum passing marks for girls?
questions.solution:
Let the maximum marks be $x$.
Then, $40\%$ of $x = 483 + 117$
$\Rightarrow \frac{40x}{100} = 600 \Rightarrow x = \left(\frac{600 \times 100}{40}\right) = 1500$.
Minimum passing marks for girls $= 35\%$ of $1500$
$= \left(\frac{35}{100} \times 1500\right) = 525$.

questions.question_text: In an examination it is required to get 40% of the aggregate marks to pass. A student get 261 marks and is declared failed by 4% marks. What are the maximum aggregate marks a student can get?
questions.solution:
Let the maximum marks be $x$.
Then, $(40-4)\%$ of $x = 261 \Rightarrow 36\%$ of $x = 261$
$\Rightarrow \frac{36x}{100} = 261 \Rightarrow x = \left(\frac{261 \times 100}{36}\right) = 725$.

questions.question_text: A candidate has to obtain minimum 33% of the total marks to pass. He got 25% of the total marks and failed by 40 marks. The maximum marks are
questions.solution:
Let the maximum marks be $x$.
Then, $33\%$ of $x - 25\%$ of $x = 40$
$\Rightarrow 8\%$ of $x = 40 \Rightarrow \frac{8x}{100} = 40$
$\Rightarrow x = \left(\frac{40 \times 100}{8}\right) = 500$.

questions.question_text: In an examination it is required to get 296 of the total maximum aggregate marks to pass. A student gets 259 marks and is decided failed. The difference of marks obtained by the student and that required to pass is 5%. What are the maximum aggregate marks a student can get?
questions.solution:
Let the maximum marks be $x$.
Then, $5\%$ of $x = 296 - 259 \Rightarrow \frac{5x}{100} = 37$
$\Rightarrow x = \left(\frac{37 \times 100}{5}\right) = 740$.

questions.question_text: Two candidates fought an election. One of them got 62% of the total votes and won by 432 votes. What is the total number of votes polled?
questions.solution:
Let the total number of votes polled be $x$.
Then, votes polled by other candidate $= (100-62)\%$ of $x$
$= 38\%$ of $x$.
$62\%$ of $x - 38\%$ of $x = 432$
$\Rightarrow \frac{24x}{100} = 432 \Rightarrow x = \left(\frac{432 \times 100}{24}\right) = 1800$.

questions.question_text: In a college election between two candidates, one candidate got 55% of the total valid votes. 15% of the votes were invalid. If the total votes were 15200, what is the number of valid votes the other candidate got?
At an election involving two candidates, 68 votes were declared invalid. The winning candidate secures 52% and wins by 98 votes. The total number of votes polled is
questions.solution:
Let the number of valid votes be $x$. Then, $52\%$ of $x - 48\%$ of $x = 98$
$\Rightarrow 4\%$ of $x = 98 \Rightarrow \frac{4}{100}x = 98$
$\Rightarrow x = 98 \times 25 = 2450$.
Total number of votes polled $= (2450 + 68) = 2518$.

questions.question_text: In a certain assembly constituency election, 80% of voters exercised their voting right and the winning candidate got elected with 65% of votes polled. What percent of total votes did he poll?
questions.solution:
Let the total number of votes be $x$.
Then, number of votes polled by wining candidate
$= 65\%$ of $80\%$ of $x$
$= \left(\frac{65}{100} \times \frac{80}{100} \times x\right) = \frac{52x}{100} = 52\%$ of $x$.
Required percentage $= 52\%$.

questions.question_text: In an election, a total of 5,00,000 voters participated. A candidate got 2,55,000 votes which was 60% of the total valid votes. What was the percentage of invalid votes?
questions.solution:
Let the number of valid votes be $x$.
Then, $60\%$ of $x = 255000 \Rightarrow x = \left(\frac{255000 \times 100}{60}\right) = 425000$.
Number of invalid votes $= (500000 - 425000) = 75000$.
Required percentage $= \left(\frac{75000}{500000} \times 100\right)\% = 15\%$.

questions.question_text: 10% of the voters did not cast their vote in an election between two candidates. 10% of the votes polled were found invalid. The successful candidate got 54% of the valid votes and won by a majority of 1620 votes. The number of voters enrolled on the voters’ list was
questions.solution:
Let the total number of voters be $x$. Then, votes polled
$= 90\%$ of $x$.
Valid votes $= 90\%$ of $(90\% \text{ of } x)$.
$54\%$ of $[90\% \text{ of } (90\% \text{ of } x)] - 46\%$ of $[90\% \text{ of } (90\% \text{ of } x)] = 1620$
$\Rightarrow 8\%$ of $[90\% \text{ of } (90\% \text{ of } x)] = 1620$
$\Rightarrow \frac{8}{100} \times \frac{90}{100} \times \frac{90}{100} \times x = 1620$
$\Rightarrow x = \left(\frac{1620 \times 100 \times 100 \times 100}{8 \times 90 \times 90}\right) = 25000$.

questions.question_text: 8% of the people eligible to vote are between 18 and 21 years of age. In an election, 85% of those eligible to vote, who were between 18 and 21, actually voted. In that election, the number of persons between 18 and 21, who actually voted, was what percent of those eligible to vote?
questions.solution:
Let the number of persons eligible to vote be $x$. Then,
Number of eligible persons between 18 and 21 $= 8\%$ of $x$.
Number of persons between 18 and 21, who voted $= 85\%$
of $(8\% \text{ of } x) = \left(\frac{85}{100} \times \frac{8}{100} \times x\right) = \frac{68}{1000}x$.
Required percentage $= \left(\frac{68}{1000} \times 100\right)\% = 6.8\%$.

questions.question_text: In an election, 30% of the voters voted for candidate A whereas 60% of the remaining voted for candidate B. The remaining voters did not vote. If the difference between those who voted for candidate A and those who did not vote was 1200, how many individuals were eligible for casting vote in that election?
questions.solution:
Let the number of persons eligible to vote be $x$.
Then, voters who voted for A $= 30\%$ of $x$.
Voters who voted for B $= 60\%$ of $(70\% \text{ of } x)$
$= \left(\frac{60}{100} \times \frac{70}{100} \times x\right) = 42\%$ of $x$.
Voters who did not vote $= [100 - (30 + 42)]\%$ of $x = 28\%$
of $x$.
$30\%$ of $x - 28\%$ of $x = 1200$
$\Rightarrow 2\%$ of $x = 1200 \Rightarrow x = \left(\frac{1200 \times 100}{2}\right) = 60000$.

questions.question_text: While purchasing one item costing ₹ 400, I had to pay the sales tax at 7% and on another costing ₹ 6400, the sales tax was 9%. What percent of the sales tax I had to pay, taking the two items together on an average?
questions.solution:
Total sales tax paid $= 7\%$ of ₹ $400 + 9\%$ of ₹ $6400$
$= ₹ \left(\frac{7}{100} \times 400 + \frac{9}{100} \times 6400\right) = ₹ (28 + 576) = ₹ 604$.
Total cost of the items $= ₹ (400 + 6400) = ₹ 6800$.
Required percentage $= \left(\frac{604}{6800} \times 100\right)\% = 8\frac{15}{17}\%$.

questions.question_text: A student secures 90%, 60% and 54% marks in test papers with 100, 150 and 200 respectively as maximum marks. The percentage of his aggregate is
questions.solution:
Total marks secured = (90% of 100 + 60% of 150 + 54% of 200)
$=  rac{90}{100}	imes100 +  rac{60}{100}	imes150 +  rac{54}{100}	imes200$
$= (90 + 90 + 108) = 288$.
Total maximum marks = (100 + 150 + 200) = 450.
Aggregate percentage = $ rac{288}{450}	imes100$ % = 64%.

questions.question_text: 1100 boys and 700 girls are examined in a test; 42% of the boys and 30% of the girls pass. The percentage of the total who failed is
questions.solution:
Total number of students = 1100 + 700 = 1800.
Number of students passed = (42% of 1100 + 30% of 700)
$= (462 + 210) = 672$.
Number of failures = 1800 - 672 = 1128.
Percentage failure = $ rac{1128}{1800}	imes100$ % = $62 rac{2}{3}$%.

questions.question_text: In a certain school, 20% of students are below 8 years of age. The number of students above 8 years of age is $ rac{2}{3}$ of the number of students of 8 years age which is 48. What is the total number of students in the school?
questions.solution:
Let the number of students be x. Then,
Number of students of or above 8 years = (100 - 20)% of x = 80% of x.
80% of x = $2 + 48$ of 48
$ rac{80}{100}x = 80 	imes 48$ (Wait, the OCR text is garbled, but the logic is 80% of x = 80, so x = 100).
$ rac{80}{100}x = 80 	imes 1 	imes 100 = 100$.

questions.question_text: In an examination, 5% of the applicants were found ineligible and 85% of the eligible candidates belonged to the general category. If 4275 eligible candidates belonged to other categories, then how many candidates applied for the examination?
questions.solution:
Let the total number of applicants be x. Number of eligible candidates = 95% of x.
Eligible candidates of other categories = 15% of (95% of x)
$=  rac{15}{100}	imes rac{95}{100}x =  rac{57}{400}x$.
$ rac{57}{400}x = 4275 	imes  rac{400}{57} = 30000$.

questions.question_text: Two students appeared at an examination. One of them secured 9 marks more than the other and his marks was 56% of the sum of their marks. The marks obtained by them are:
questions.solution:
Let their marks be (x + 9) and x.
Then, $x + 9 =  rac{56}{100}(x + 9 + x)$
$25(x + 9) = 14(2x + 9) 	imes 3x = 99 	imes x = 33$.
So, their marks are 42 and 33.

questions.question_text: At a special sale, 5 tickets can be purchased for the price of 3 tickets. If 5 tickets are purchased at the sale, the amount saved will be what percent of the original price of the 5 tickets?
questions.solution:
Let the original price of each ticket be ₹ 100.
Then, original price of 5 tickets = ₹ 500.
Sale price of 5 tickets = ₹ 300.
Amount saved = ₹ (500 - 300) = ₹ 200.
Required percentage = $ rac{200}{500}	imes100$ % = 40%.

questions.question_text: In September 2009, the sales of a product were $ rac{2}{3}$ of that in July 2009. In November 2009, the sales of the product were higher by 5% as compared to September 2009. How much is the percentage of increase in sales in November 2009 with respect to the base figure in July 2009?
questions.solution:
Let the total sales in July 2009 be ₹ x. Then, sales in September 2009 = ₹ $ rac{2}{3}x$.
Sales in November 2009 = 105% of ₹ $ rac{2}{3}x$
$= ₹  rac{105}{100}	imes rac{2}{3}x = ₹  rac{7}{10}x$.
Decrease in sales = ₹ $(x -  rac{7}{10}x) =  rac{3}{10}x$.
Decrease % = $ rac{3}{10}x 	imes  rac{1}{x} 	imes 100$ % = 30%.

questions.question_text: If earth’s rotational motion increases by 12%, then the relation between a day and hours will be
questions.solution:
Earth takes 1 day or 24 hours to complete one rotation.
Let the time taken to complete one rotation with increased speed be x hours.

questions.question_text: A and B are two fixed points 5 cm apart and C is a point on AB such that AC is 3 cm. If the length of AC is increased by 6%, the length of CB is decreased by
questions.solution:
A 3 cm C 2 cm B
Original length of AB = 5 cm.
Original length of AC = 3 cm.
Original length of CB = (5 - 3) cm = 2 cm.
New length of AC = 106% of 3 cm = $ rac{106}{100}	imes3$ cm = 3.18 cm.
New length of CB = (5 - 3.18) cm = 1.82 cm.
Decrease in length of CB = (2 - 1.82) cm = 0.18 cm.
Decrease % = $ rac{0.18}{2}	imes100$ % = 9%.

questions.passage_text:
On time x%
Up to 15 minutes delayed 43%
15-30 minutes delayed 17%
30-60 minutes delayed 12%
More than 60 minutes delayed 3%
The chart above describes departures from a certain airport on a certain day. If 1200 flights were delayed, how many flights departed on time?
questions.question_text: The chart above describes departures from a certain airport on a certain day. If 1200 flights were delayed, how many flights departed on time?
questions.solution:
Percentage of delayed flights = (43 + 17 + 12 + 3)% = 75%.
Percentage of on-time flights = (100 - 75)% = 25%.
Let the total number of flights be x.
Then, 75% of x = 1200 $	imes x =  rac{1200	imes100}{75} = 1600$.
Number of on-time flights = 25% of 1600 = 400.

questions.question_text: A shopkeeper has a certain number of eggs of which 5% are found to be broken. He sells 93% of the remainder and still has 266 eggs left. How many eggs did he originally have?
questions.solution:
Let the total number of eggs originally be x.
Then, number of eggs which are intact = (100 - 5)% of x = $ rac{95}{100}x =  rac{19}{20}x$.
Number of eggs left unsold = (100 - 93)% of $ rac{19}{20}x$
$=  rac{7}{100}	imes rac{19}{20}x$.
$ rac{7}{100}	imes rac{19}{20}x = 266 	imes x =  rac{266	imes100	imes20}{7	imes19} = 4000$.

questions.question_text: Ganpat went to fruit market with a certain amount of money. With this money he can buy either 50 oranges or 40 mangoes. He retains 10% of the money for taxi fare. If he buys 20 mangoes, the number of oranges he can buy is
questions.solution:
Suppose Ganpat has ₹ x.
Then, cost of 1 orange = ₹ $ rac{x}{50}$; cost of 1 mango = ₹ $ rac{x}{40}$.
Money left after paying taxi fare = 90% of ₹ x = ₹ $ rac{9}{10}x$.
Money spent in buying 20 mangoes = ₹ $ rac{x}{40}	imes20 = ₹  rac{x}{2}$.
Money left = ₹ $( rac{9}{10}x -  rac{x}{2}) =  rac{4}{10}x =  rac{2}{5}x$.
Number of oranges that can be bought = $ rac{2}{5}x 	imes  rac{50}{x} = 20$.

questions.question_text: A 14.4 kg gas cylinder runs for 104 hours when the smaller burner on the gas stove is fully opened while it runs for 80 hours when the larger burner on the gas stove is fully opened. Which of these values is the closest to the percentage difference in the usage of gas per hour, of the smaller burner over the larger burner?
questions.solution:
Consumption of gas in the smaller burner in 1 hour = $ rac{14.4}{104} =  rac{9}{65}$ kg.
Consumption of gas in the larger burner in 1 hour = $ rac{14.4}{80} =  rac{9}{50}$ kg.
Difference in consumption = $( rac{9}{50} -  rac{9}{65}) =  rac{27}{650}$ kg.
Required percentage difference = $ rac{27}{650}	imes rac{50}{9}	imes100$ % = 23.07%.
